blurb: These verses cut through every polite evasion: to deny the blood is to lose life itself. The apostle writes not to win an argument but to keep hearts joined to the one who spilled his life for them. Stand here long enough and the word “blood” stops sounding crude; it becomes the only honest name for costly love. Let today’s small denials—of pain, of need, of dependence—shrink before the crimson clarity that rescues us.

verses.text: Even the heavenly beings and the glory of the angels and the rulers visible and invisible, if they believe not in the blood of Christ who is God, judgment awaiteth them also.
He that receiveth let him receive.
Let not office puff up any man;
